questions.question: Prove that equal chords of a circle subtend equal angles at the center.
questions.answer: Let AB and CD be equal chords of a circle with center O. Join OA, OB, OC, OD. Using triangle congruence (SAS), ∆OAB ≅ ∆OCD, hence ∠AOB = ∠COD.

questions.question: Prove that the perpendicular from the center to a chord bisects the chord.
questions.answer: Let AB be a chord and O the center. Draw perpendicular OM to AB. Triangles ∆OMA ≅ ∆OMB (RHS), so AM = MB; hence chord is bisected.

questions.question: Prove that chords equidistant from the center are equal.
questions.answer: Let AB and CD be chords at equal distance from center O. Draw perpendiculars OM and ON to AB and CD. Using RHS congruence of triangles, AB = CD.

questions.question: Prove that the line joining the center to the midpoint of a chord is perpendicular to the chord.
questions.answer: Let AB be a chord and M its midpoint. Join OM. Using congruent triangles ∆OMA ≅ ∆OMB, ∠OMB = 90°; hence OM ⟂ AB.

questions.question: Prove that the angle subtended by a chord at the center is double the angle subtended on the circle.
questions.answer: Let chord AB subtend ∠AOB at center and ∠ACB on circle. Using isosceles triangles and angle sum properties, ∠AOB = 2∠ACB.

questions.question: Prove that the angle in a semicircle is a right angle.
questions.answer: Let diameter AB subtend angle ∠ACB. Triangles ∆ABC is right-angled at C by Pythagoras theorem or using circle theorem.

questions.question: Prove that opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral sum to 180°.
questions.answer: Let ABCD be cyclic quadrilateral. Using angle subtended by same chord, ∠A + ∠C = 180°, ∠B + ∠D = 180°.

questions.question: Prove that tangents drawn from an external point are equal.
questions.answer: Let P be an external point and PA, PB tangents. Join OP. Triangles ∆OPA ≅ ∆OPB (RHS), hence PA = PB.

questions.question: Prove that a radius drawn to the tangent at the point of contact is perpendicular to the tangent.
questions.answer: Let tangent touch at A and radius OA. Assume not perpendicular, lead to contradiction using triangle properties. Hence OA ⟂ tangent.

questions.question: Prove that angles in the same segment of a circle are equal.
questions.answer: Let chord AB subtend ∠ACD and ∠AED in the same segment. Using properties of isosceles triangles and circle, ∠ACD = ∠AED.

questions.question: Prove that the perpendicular bisector of a chord passes through the center.
questions.answer: Let AB be a chord and M its midpoint. Draw perpendicular from M. By congruence of triangles, line passes through O, the center.

questions.question: Prove that a quadrilateral is cyclic if a pair of opposite angles sum to 180°.
questions.answer: Let ∠A + ∠C = 180°. Draw circle through three vertices; the fourth vertex lies on circle, making quadrilateral cyclic.

questions.question: Prove that tangents drawn at ends of a diameter are parallel.
questions.answer: Tangents at ends of diameter are perpendicular to radius; both perpendicular to same line, hence tangents are parallel.

questions.question: Prove that the angle between a tangent and a chord equals the angle in the alternate segment.
questions.answer: Using tangent-radius perpendicularity and triangle angle properties, angle between tangent and chord = angle in alternate segment.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral is 180° using exterior angle theorem.
questions.answer: Extend side, apply exterior angle theorem to show ∠A + ∠C = 180°, ∠B + ∠D = 180°.

questions.question: Prove that the product of lengths of two segments of a secant equals the square of the tangent from the same external point.
questions.answer: Using triangle similarity between tangents and secant segments, PT² = PA·PB where P is external point, T tangent, AB secant.

questions.question: Prove that two tangents drawn from an external point form equal angles with the line joining the point to the center.
questions.answer: Triangles formed by tangents and radii are congruent (RHS), hence angles with line to center equal.

questions.question: Prove that the radius divides a chord into two equal parts if it passes through the midpoint.
questions.answer: Draw radius to midpoint, form two triangles, use congruence to show chord is bisected.

questions.question: Prove that the perpendicular from center to chord bisects the chord using coordinates.
questions.answer: Place center at origin, chord along x-axis, calculate midpoint, slope of perpendicular line, show it passes through midpoint.

questions.question: Prove that equal chords are equidistant from the center using coordinates.
questions.answer: Place chords along parallel lines, calculate distances from origin, show distances equal for equal chords.

questions.question: Prove that angles subtended by same chord on same side are equal using isosceles triangles.
questions.answer: Triangles formed by chord and points on circle are isosceles. Using base angles equality, angles subtended are equal.

questions.question: Prove that a tangent and radius at point of contact are perpendicular using Pythagoras theorem.
questions.answer: Construct triangle with radius, tangent, and line from center to external point. Apply Pythagoras theorem to show perpendicularity.

questions.question: Prove that a cyclic quadrilateral is inscribed in a circle.
questions.answer: Construct circle through three vertices, show fourth vertex lies on circle, using opposite angles sum to 180°.

questions.question: Prove that in a circle, chords equally distant from the center subtend equal angles at the center.
questions.answer: Draw perpendiculars from center to chords, use congruence of triangles formed, show angles at center equal.

questions.question: Prove that the sum of opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral using circle properties.
questions.answer: Using angles subtended by same chord on circle, apply angle properties to show opposite angles sum to 180°.
